diagrams.title: PRNG Algorithm Block Diagram
diagrams.description: Shows the internal structure and data flow of a generic PRNG algorithm with seed input, state register, transition function, and output function
elements: State Transition Function (arrow pointing to next state)
Output Function (arrow pointing to random output)
Feedback loop from new state to state register
diagrams.purpose: Visualizes how the PRNG algorithm components interact to generate pseudorandom sequences, showing the iterative nature of the process

diagrams.title: State Space and Period Relationship
diagrams.description: Diagram showing relationship between state space size and maximum possible period using a state machine representation
elements: Example with n=3 showing 8 possible states
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates why state space size determines maximum period and helps students understand the fundamental limit on PRNG sequence length

diagrams.title: Cryptographic PRNG Security Properties
diagrams.description: Venn diagram or table showing properties distinguishing cryptographic PRNGs from statistical PRNGs
diagrams.purpose: Clarifies the additional requirements for cryptographic security beyond mere statistical randomness
